content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game. At the start of the game, there are NNN piles of stones, the iii-th of which has AiA_iAi​ stones.

Alice and Bob alternate moves. On their move, a player does the following:


Choose one pile of stones, and discard the remaining N−1N-1N−1 piles.
Partition the stones in the chosen pile into **exactly** NNN new piles of stones, each containing at least 111 stone. Note that this means that the number of piles is always NNN.
The player who cannot make a move loses. Determine the winner of the game if Alice moves first.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, the number of piles.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For every test case print exactly one line containing the answer — either "Alice" or "Bob" (without the quotes).
You can output Alice and Bob in any case (for example, the strings `BOb`, `BOB`, `bob` and `boB` will be recognized as a positive response)
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 3⋅105 3\cdot 10^53⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first move, Alice can select the first pile and divide it into 333 piles of sizes 2,22, 22,2, and 111. After this, Bob cannot make a move since each pile has less than 333 stones.
